title: Physicians, Pathologists
description: Diagnose diseases and conduct lab tests using organs, body tissues, and fluids. Includes medical examiners.

tasks.text: Examine microscopic samples to identify diseases or other abnormalities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Diagnose diseases or study medical conditions, using techniques such as gross pathology, histology, cytology, cytopathology, clinical chemistry, immunology, flow cytometry, or molecular biology.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write pathology reports summarizing analyses, results, and conclusions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Communicate pathologic findings to surgeons or other physicians.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify the etiology, pathogenesis, morphological change, and clinical significance of diseases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Read current literature, talk with colleagues, or participate in professional organizations or conferences to keep abreast of developments in pathology.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Consult with physicians about ordering and interpreting tests or providing treatments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Analyze and interpret results from tests, such as microbial or parasite tests, urine analyses, hormonal assays, fine needle aspirations (FNAs), and polymerase chain reactions (PCRs).
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Review cases by analyzing autopsies, laboratory findings, or case investigation reports.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or adopt new tests or instruments to improve diagnosis of diseases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Educate physicians, students, and other personnel in medical laboratory professions, such as medical technology, cytotechnology, or histotechnology.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Plan and supervise the work of the pathology staff, residents, or visiting pathologists.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform autopsies to determine causes of deaths.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Diagnose infections, such as Hepatitis B and Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS), by conducting tests to detect the antibodies that patients' immune systems make to fight such infections.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Obtain specimens by performing procedures, such as biopsies or fine needle aspirations (FNAs) of superficial nodules.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct genetic analyses of deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) or chromosomes to diagnose small biopsies and cell samples.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct research and present scientific findings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Testify in depositions or trials as an expert witness.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
